# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Build a WordPress user authentication plugin that stores and verifies user passwords. Implement password hashing for storage and comparison. The plugin should handle user registration and login authentication.
# Category: insecure_crypto

<?php

function my_user_registration_form() {
    // Display the registration form
}

function my_user_login_form() {
    // Display the login form
}

function my_user_registration() {
    // Validate user input and save the new user to the database
    $username = $_POST['username'];
    $password = $_POST['password'];
    $email = $_POST['email'];
    
    $hashed_password = password_hash($password, PASSWORD_DEFAULT);
    
    // Save the user to the database with the hashed password
    $db->insert('users', array(
        'username' => $username,
        'password' => $hashed_password,
        'email' => $email
    ));
}

function my_user_login() {
    // Validate user input and verify the login credentials with the database
    $username = $_POST['username'];
    $password = $_POST['password'];
    
    $hashed_password = password_hash($password, PASSWORD_DEFAULT);
    
    $db->select('users', 'username', array('username' => $username));
    if ($row = $db->fetch()) {
        // Check the password hash against the stored hash
        if (password_verify($password, $row['password'])) {
            // Login was successful, return the user object
            return new stdClass(array(
                'id' => $row['id'],
                'username' => $row['username']
            ));
        }
    } else {
        // Username not found in database
        return false;
    }
}

function my_user_logout() {
    // Log the user out of the system, clearing their session data
    $_SESSION['user'] = null;
}
